presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
The documents address distinct compliance controls: one focuses on vendor evidence completeness for a quarterly review, while the other governs privileged access with strict operational and verification requirements. Both emphasize documentation rigor but target different regulatory domains.

- Document 1 concerns a synthetic evidence register for vendor control remediation, requiring resolution of missing items like EV-002 to close the control.  
- Document 2 approves limited privileged access for specific administrators under a pilot, mandating scoping, logging, quarterly reviews, and vendor confirmation.  
- Document 1 is a retrospective compliance artifact; Document 2 is a prospective access authorization with ongoing governance.  
- Document 1 mandates evidence provenance and archival; Document 2 requires continuous logging integration and vendor validation.  
- Document 1 centers on control closure through evidence completeness; Document 2 centers on access governance through periodic recertification.  
- Document 1 applies to vendor management processes; Document 2 applies to internal privileged access management.
key_highlights: The documents address distinct compliance controls: one focuses on vendor evidence completeness for a quarterly review, while the other governs privileged access with strict operational and verification requirements. Both emphasize documentation rigor but target different regulatory domains.
Document 1 concerns a synthetic evidence register for vendor control remediation, requiring resolution of missing items like EV-002 to close the control.
Document 2 approves limited privileged access for specific administrators under a pilot, mandating scoping, logging, quarterly reviews, and vendor confirmation.
Document 1 is a retrospective compliance artifact; Document 2 is a prospective access authorization with ongoing governance.
Document 1 mandates evidence provenance and archival; Document 2 requires continuous logging integration and vendor validation.
Document 1 centers on control closure through evidence completeness; Document 2 centers on access governance through periodic recertification.
